Lawful Basis / Legal Basis for Processing
Confidentiality Obligations of Processor Personnel (GDPR Art. 28(3)(b))
positiveClauses: Clause 1 — Scope: correctly identifies controller-processor relationship and general purpose of processing

results.label: DPA — international transfer with no mechanism
missingClauses: International transfer mechanism (SCCs / EU-US DPF / TIA)
Confidentiality obligations on Processor personnel (Art. 28(3)(b) GDPR)
DPIA and prior consultation assistance (Art. 28(3)(f) GDPR)
Return or deletion of data upon termination details and certification (Art. 28(3)(g) GDPR)
Lawful basis and documented instructions requirement
positiveClauses: Data Retention — includes defined deletion timeline post-termination (Clause 4)

issues: Red flags: Does not flag the EU-US transfer without an SCC or adequacy mechanism | Does not flag health data as special category requiring additional protection
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the EU-US transfer without an SCC or adequacy mechanism

results.label: DPA — well drafted, minimal issues expected
missingClauses: Description of Processing / Annex I (subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, data types, data subject categories)
positiveClauses: Clause 2 — Scope and Instructions: properly limits processing to documented instructions including transfer restrictions per Art. 28(3)(a)
Clause 3 — Confidentiality: meets Art. 28(3)(b) confidentiality commitment requirement
Clause 4 — Security (Article 32): strong and specific — names AES-256, TLS 1.3, access controls, and regular testing, exceeding the typical Art. 32 minimum
Clause 5 — Sub-processors: compliant with Art. 28(2)/(4) — requires prior written authorisation, lists current sub-processors, and provides 30-day advance notice of changes
Clause 6 — Data Subject Rights: responsive assistance within 5 business days covering Arts. 15-22
Clause 7 — Breach Notification: 24-hour notification window is more protective than the GDPR's 'without undue delay' standard — well drafted
Clause 8 — International Transfers: correctly references Commission Decision 2021/914 SCCs for EEA transfers
Clause 9 — Deletion: compliant with Art. 28(3)(g) — 30-day deletion/return with written certification
Clause 10 — Audit Rights: meets Art. 28(3)(h) audit obligation with reasonable notice and frequency terms

results.label: DPA — HIPAA context, missing BAA obligations
missingClauses: Sub-Contractor / Downstream Business Associate Obligations (45 CFR § 164.504(e)(2)(ii)(D))
Individual Rights — Access, Amendment, and Accounting of Disclosures (45 CFR §§ 164.524, 164.526, 164.528)
Audit Rights and HHS Access (45 CFR § 164.504(e)(2)(ii)(H))
Material Breach Cure and Termination Rights (45 CFR § 164.504(e)(2)(iii))
HIPAA Definitions (PHI, Breach, Security Incident, Unsecured PHI, Required by Law)
positiveClauses: Section 1 — Services: Clearly identifies the nature of services (cloud-based EHR storage) and the categories of PHI processed

issues: Red flags: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards

No-sale commitment (Clause 2 includes prohibition on sale, though incomplete)
